name: hanzi-in-chrome
description: any llm browsing for you in chrome

commit.message:
Update Lemon Squeezy checkout URL to production

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update README license to Polyform Noncommercial

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Change license from MIT to Polyform Noncommercial 1.0.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump extension to 1.0.0 for launch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

README: clear credential setup guide for 3 user types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

README: remove context param, add credential info

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite README for launch: user-facing copy, npx setup, pricing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add license enforcement to MCP server, complete v2 agent loop rewrite

MCP server now enforces the same 100-task free tier as the extension,
using filesystem storage (~/.hanzi-in-chrome/mcp-license.json and
mcp-usage.json). Supports auto-activation via HANZI_IN_CHROME_LICENSE_KEY
env var and 7-day offline grace period for Pro users.

Also includes the v2 architecture: server-side agent loop driving LLM
calls directly, with the extension acting as a remote tool executor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename to Hanzi in Chrome, add licensing, improve MCP onboarding

- Full rename from "LLM in Chrome" to "Hanzi in Chrome" across all source,
  docs, native host IDs, package names, env vars, and URLs
- Add license-manager.js: 100 free tasks, Pro via Lemon Squeezy ($29)
- Gate task execution in service worker with license check
- Add License tab to Settings modal (status, activation, buy link)
- Forward HANZI_IN_CHROME_LICENSE_KEY env var from MCP server
- Add relay status_query for extension connectivity check
- Add pre-flight check + auto-open Chrome Web Store if extension disconnected
- Improve MCP tool descriptions with WHEN TO USE / WHEN NOT TO USE
- Change timeout from 10min to 5min, make timeout a non-error status
- Remove legacy orchestrator, agents, memory, and LLM client code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clarify README setup for two user types, bump to 0.3.0

Restructured Quick Start for extension-only users (steps 1-2) and
MCP users (steps 1-3). Explained credential bridge inline instead
of jargon. API key users skip the bridge entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite README for MCP-first launch, bump extension to 0.3.0

Repositioned README to target Claude Code, Cursor, and MCP tool users.
Added collapsible setup sections, use-case examples, and comparison table.
Bumped extension version for Web Store republish.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify MCP server: blocking tool calls, remove orchestrator

- browser_start now blocks until task completes (no polling needed)
- browser_message also blocks until agent finishes acting on it
- Remove orchestrator, planning agent, and explorer agent dependencies
- Simplify session file schema (remove stale agent trace fields)
- Fix cross-session event leak in CLI (filter by activeSessionId)
- Fix [object Object] in completion logs
- Restore sidepanel hooks/config that were prematurely deleted
- Add research/ to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update extension tools, providers, MCP bridge, and native host

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add WebSocket relay, direct LLM routing via ccproxy, and extension keepalive

Replace native host polling with WebSocket relay (ws://localhost:7862) for
real-time bidirectional communication between MCP server and extension.
Planning/Explorer agents now call ccproxy directly via HTTP instead of
routing through the extension. Extension gains alarm-based keepalive and
Codex→ccproxy auto-fallback for the browser agent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 3-agent orchestration, parallel execution, and MCP server refactor

Introduces a multi-agent architecture (Planning → Explorer → Browser) with
per-session browser windows, concurrency limits, Mem0 memory integration,
model tiers, get_info tool, and native host rename to native-bridge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add popup tracking for OAuth flows and new windows

When agent clicks something that opens a popup (OAuth, payment, etc.):
- CDP Target domain detects new popup via Target.targetCreated
- Agent's attention shifts to popup (tabId updates, previous pushed to stack)
- Debugger attaches to popup for interaction
- MCP server notified of focus shift

When popup closes:
- Target.targetDestroyed fires
- Agent returns to previous tab (pop from stack)
- MCP server notified of return

This enables agents to complete OAuth flows without losing track
of the original task.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add concurrency limit for parallel browser agents

- Limit concurrent sessions to 5 by default (prevents Chrome overload)
- Configurable via LLM_IN_CHROME_MAX_SESSIONS environment variable
- Returns clear error with guidance when limit is reached
- Fixes issue where too many parallel agents caused timeouts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve OAuth error messages with actionable guidance

- Parse OAuth error responses for specific error types
- Provide clear, actionable messages for common failures:
  - invalid_grant: session expired
  - invalid_client: reinstall needed
  - unauthorized_client: re-authenticate
  - server_error: temporary API issue
- Add structured error info (errorType, action, hint) for MCP server

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add anti-bot human-like mouse movement for protected sites

- Domain skills can now specify antiBot: true for sites with bot detection
- CDP helper implements Bezier curve mouse movement with natural timing
- Computer tool automatically uses human-like behavior on antiBot domains
- Adds realistic delays and movement patterns to avoid detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve install scripts with dev/prod extension ID support

- Add native host install instructions to README
- Support both production and development extension IDs
- Note about custom extension IDs for unpacked extensions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Chrome Web Store support

- Published to Chrome Web Store with ID: iklpkemlmbhemkiojndpbhoakgikpmcd
- Update install scripts to use published ID as default
- Add Chrome Web Store badge and install link to README
- Fix native-host-wrapper.sh path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Add privacy policy for Chrome Web Store

Add MCP Server section to Preact settings UI

- Add MCP integration section with install command
- Style the install command as copyable code block
- Mark old sidepanel.html as deprecated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add one-liner installer and MCP integration link in UI

- Add curl-able install.sh for easy native host setup
- Add MCP Server section to extension settings UI
- Update README with one-liner install instructions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update README with domain skills and Keychain support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix debugger manager for parallel execution

- Change from single-tab tracking to multi-tab tracking (Set-based)
- Remove code that detaches from previous tab when attaching to new tab
- Chrome allows multiple debugger attachments to different tabs
- Update detachDebugger to accept optional tabId for per-tab detach
- MCP tasks now only detach their own tab, not all tabs

This was the root cause of parallel execution failure - Task B was
detaching the debugger from Task A's tab.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix parallel execution: don't await startMcpTaskInternal

Key changes:
- Don't await startMcpTaskInternal - fire and forget for parallel exec
- Add per-session abortController to session object
- Use session's abortController for cancellation instead of global

This allows multiple MCP tasks to run concurrently without blocking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enable parallel execution for MCP tasks

Architecture changes:
- Replace global `agentSessionActive` boolean with `activeSessions` Set
- Add `isAnySessionActive()` function for compatibility
- Extend MCP session object with per-session state:
  - screenshots[], debugLog[], steps[], openedTabs, startTime
- Use per-session screenshots in runAgentLoop via mcpSession param
- Each session now tracked independently in activeSessions Set

This enables multiple browser automation tasks to run concurrently,
each with isolated state (screenshots, logs, cancellation).

UI-triggered tasks use special 'ui-task' session ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add macOS Keychain support and LinkedIn domain skills

- Add macOS Keychain fallback for Claude OAuth credentials in native host
  (Claude Code stores credentials in Keychain, not plain text file)
- Add LinkedIn domain skill: Easy Apply forms have pre-filled contact info
  that cannot be modified - agent should accept and proceed
- Add usage tracking manager for API call and task statistics
- Improve form-tool with better field detection and error handling
- Enhance MCP bridge with debug logging
- Fix accessibility tree edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update README with MCP server docs and session continuation

- Add MCP server section to main README with setup instructions
- Document session continuation feature in MCP server README
- Add usage tracking field to task logs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add per-session state for MCP tasks and improve response format

- Each MCP session now has isolated chat history (parallel tasks don't interfere)
- Sessions persist after completion, allowing continuation via browser_message
- Auto-cleanup of sessions after 1 hour to prevent memory leaks
- Simplified response format: status shows current_activity and full step history
- Fixed screenshot tool to return image directly instead of "check status"
- Updated tool descriptions with usage guidelines
- Fixed callLLMSimple to use user's selected model instead of hardcoded one

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add MCP server for high-level browser automation

Features:
- MCP server with 5 tools: browser_start, browser_message, browser_status,
  browser_stop, browser_screenshot
- File-based IPC for cross-process communication (MCP ↔ Extension)
- OAuth auto-refresh on 401 errors (like ccproxy-api)
- Fix UI text not accumulating (text sent before tool execution)

Architecture:
- MCP server spawns native host, communicates via stdin/stdout
- Native host writes MCP commands to ~/.llm-in-chrome/mcp-inbox.json
- Extension polls inbox, executes tasks, writes results to outbox
- MCP server polls outbox for status updates

This enables Claude Code (or any MCP client) to delegate web tasks to
the browser agent without exposing low-level browser primitives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add OAuth token auto-refresh on 401 errors

When the native host receives a 401 from Anthropic API:
1. Attempts to refresh the token using the refresh_token
2. Updates ~/.claude/.credentials.json with new tokens
3. Notifies the extension to update Chrome storage
4. Retries the original request with the new token

This matches ccproxy-api behavior for seamless token management.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Preact frontend-backend message type mismatches

- SET_CONFIG → SAVE_CONFIG (model switching was broken)
- LOGOUT → OAUTH_LOGOUT (Claude logout was broken)
- LOGOUT_CODEX → CODEX_LOGOUT (Codex logout was broken)
- CLEAR_HISTORY → CLEAR_CONVERSATION (clear chat was broken)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Add demo section and simplify README

Preact UI rewrite with Codex integration and polish

- Rewrite sidepanel UI in Preact for better maintainability
- Add Vite build system for the new UI
- Integrate Codex (ChatGPT Pro) subscription support
- Fix tab activation for Google Docs and similar apps
- Remove Claude-specific "Ask before acting" feature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
WIP: Codex/ChatGPT Pro integration (experimental)

Remove unused files

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Major refactor: Provider architecture, OAuth, and LLM-agnostic codebase

Architecture:

- Add CDP helper and supporting utilities

Features:

- Implement conversation compaction for long sessions

Code Quality:
- Make codebase LLM-agnostic (generic variable names, clean comments)
- Add ESLint and Prettier configuration

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite README and add DevPost submission in casual, accessible style

- Rewrote README with conversational tone, lowercase headers, no bullet points
- Emphasizes "open-source Claude in Chrome" positioning
- Added DEV_POST.md for hackathon submission
- Mentions Deckathon dropout challenge, job applications, Gmail automation

Makes the project more approachable and easier to read.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor codebase to be LLM-agnostic

Removed Claude-specific references throughout the codebase to emphasize the project's LLM-agnostic nature:

- Renamed functions: callClaude -> callLLM, callClaudeSimple -> callLLMSimple, resizeScreenshotForClaude -> resizeScreenshot
- Renamed variables: __claudeElementMap -> __elementMap, __claudeRefCounter -> __refCounter, __claudeElementOffsets -> __elementOffsets
- Updated comments to remove "Claude in Chrome", "like Claude", "matching Claude" references
- Replaced "Claude" with generic "agent" or "assistant" in system prompts
- Updated file headers and documentation comments
- Improved code quality with cleaner, more generic terminology

This makes it clear the extension works with any LLM (Claude, GPT, Gemini, or custom).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Delete GITHUB_SETTINGS.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CAPTCHA solver and improve dropout flow

CAPTCHA Changes:
- Rewrite solve_captcha to call /dropout API directly with token
- Fix entropy values (150 instead of 25) to meet server requirements
- Add brute force support for 'sun' and 'logos' CAPTCHAs
- Restrict solve_captcha to /dropout page only (prevents breaking other CAPTCHAs)

Dropout Flow Fixes:
- Document exact activity thresholds and countdown requirements
- Add critical note about activity during countdown
- Improve error handling for 401 (session expired) and entropy failures

Configuration:
- Change default model from Sonnet to Opus 4.5 (claude-opus-4-5-20251101)
- Add userSkills support in api.js loadConfig
- Load config at start of runAgentLoop to ensure skills are available

Domain Skills:
- Add user skills override functionality (getDomainSkills now merges user + built-in)
- User skills can override or extend built-in skills per domain
- Add logging for loaded domain skills

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add new tools:

  - solve_captcha: Auto-solve hackathon CAPTCHAs

- Update system prompt with popup window detection guidance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add filePath support for direct local file uploads

For local Chrome extensions, users can provide the absolute file path
directly instead of needing to download from URL.

Usage:
  file_upload({
    selector: "input[type=file]",
    filePath: "/Users/name/Documents/resume.pdf",
    tabId: 123
  })

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add file_upload tool for uploading files to input elements

Features:
- Upload files from URL (downloads first, then sets on input)
- Upload files from base64 data
- Find file input by ref (from read_page) or CSS selector
- Uses CDP DOM.setFileInputFiles for reliable file setting
- Added generateUniqueSelector helper for element targeting

Usage:
  file_upload({
    ref: "ref_123",  // or selector: "input[type=file]"
    fileUrl: "https://example.com/resume.pdf",
    fileName: "resume.pdf",
    tabId: 123
  })

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix missing closing brace in detachDebugger function

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor: Extract modules from service-worker.js

Split large service-worker.js into focused modules:
- modules/api.js - Claude API communication, config, abort handling
- modules/domain-skills.js - Site-specific guidance for 14 domains
- modules/keys.js - Keyboard definitions and key press utilities
- modules/system-prompt.js - Agent system prompt builder

service-worker.js reduced from 2188 to 1640 lines (-25%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: Browser Agent extension

AI-powered browser automation assistant based on Claude in Chrome.

Features:
- Claude API integration with streaming responses
- Computer use tools (screenshot, click, type, scroll, etc.)
- Tab management with automatic tab groups
- Visual indicators (pulsing glow when agent is active)
- Domain-specific skills for Gmail, GitHub, LinkedIn, etc.
- Plan approval workflow for task execution
- Task logging with screenshots saved to downloads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

readme_html:
Stop being your AI's intern.

Your AI agent can't use a browser. So it makes you do it — open this URL, click that button, paste the result back. Over and over.

Hanzi in Chrome is a browser agent that lives inside your MCP. It takes over your real Chrome — your logins, your sessions — and browses, clicks, and fills forms autonomously.

Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Codex CLI, and anything that supports MCP.

Demo

Why not Playwright MCP / Browser Use?

Those tools give your AI a new, empty browser. Every click is a separate tool call. Logging in is a nightmare.

This gives your AI your actual Chrome — already logged into Gmail, GitHub, Jira, everything. One tool call, entire task delegated.

# Other tools: 50+ tool calls, one click at a time
ai: click login button
ai: type username
ai: type password
ai: click submit
ai: wait for page load
ai: click menu
... (you get the idea)

# Hanzi in Chrome: 1 tool call, done
ai: browser_start("Log into Jira and summarize my open tickets")
ai: → "You have 3 open tickets..."

Setup

1. Install the Chrome extension

Install from Chrome Web Store

2. Add the MCP server to your AI tool

The agent needs an LLM to drive the browser. Pick one:

  • Claude Pro/Max subscriber? You're set — it uses your claude login automatically.
  • Codex subscriber? Run codex login and you're set.
  • API key? Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in your environment.
claude mcp add browser -- npx -y hanzi-in-chrome-mcp

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "browser": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "hanzi-in-chrome-mcp"]
    }
  }
}

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "browser": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "hanzi-in-chrome-mcp"]
    }
  }
}

That's it. Your AI can now use your browser.

What you can do

Logged-in tasks — it's your Chrome, you're already authenticated

browser_start("Go to Gmail and unsubscribe from all marketing emails from the last week")

Form filling

browser_start("Apply for the senior engineer position on careers.acme.com. Name: Jane Doe, Email: jane@example.com, Experience: 10 years Python")

Multi-step workflows

browser_start("Log into my bank and download last month's statement")

Parallel tasks — each runs in its own browser window

browser_start("Search for flights to Tokyo on Google Flights")
browser_start("Check hotel prices in Shibuya on Booking.com")
browser_start("Look up JR Pass costs")

Follow-ups — the browser window stays open

session = browser_start("Find AI engineer jobs on LinkedIn in San Francisco")
browser_message(session_id, "Apply to the first one using my profile")

Pricing

Free: 100 tasks, no credit card needed.

Pro: $29 one-time for unlimited tasks. Buy Pro

After purchasing, set your license key:

export HANZI_IN_CHROME_LICENSE_KEY=your-key-here

MCP Tools

Tool Description
browser_start Run a task. Blocks until complete, returns the result.
browser_message Send follow-up instructions to an existing session.
browser_status Check progress of active tasks.
browser_stop Stop a task.
browser_screenshot Capture current browser state as PNG.

How it works

Your AI tool (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.)
        ↓ MCP Protocol
   Hanzi MCP Server (runs locally via npx)
        ↓ WebSocket
   Chrome Extension (your real browser)
        ↓ Autonomous agent
   Websites (with your logins)
  1. Your AI calls browser_start with a task
  2. The MCP server sends it to the Chrome extension
  3. The browser agent handles all interaction autonomously
  4. Results flow back to your AI

Comparison

Hanzi in Chrome Playwright MCP Browser Use
Abstraction Task-level (1 call) Action-level (50+ calls) Action-level (50+ calls)
Browser Your real Chrome New headless browser New Chromium instance
Logged-in sites Already authenticated Must handle auth Must handle auth
Setup Chrome extension + npx Playwright + Node Python + pip
Parallel tasks Built-in Manual Manual

Development

git clone https://github.com/hanzili/hanzi-in-chrome.git
cd mcp-server && npm install && npm run build

License

Polyform Noncommercial 1.0.0
